annotations.label: Locally small category

anchor.snippet: Let C be a locally small category

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Category` typeclass already makes hom-classes types (locally small); `LocallySmall.{w}` is the relative w-small generalization with `hom_small : ∀ X Y, Small.{w} (X ⟶ Y)`.

annotations.label: Covariant Hom functor Hom(A,–)

anchor.snippet: This is a covariant functor given by

annotations.note: `coyoneda.obj (op A) : C ⥤ Type` is exactly the covariant hom functor Hom(A,–).

annotations.label: Contravariant Hom functor Hom(–,B)

anchor.snippet: Hom(–, B ) maps each object X in C to the set of morphisms

annotations.note: `yoneda.obj B : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ Type` is exactly the contravariant hom functor Hom(–,B).

annotations.label: Functor of points

anchor.snippet: also called the functor of points of the object

annotations.note: The functor of points of an object A is the representable presheaf `yoneda.obj A = Hom(–,A)`, though Mathlib has no dedicated 'functor of points' name.

annotations.label: Naturality of Hom functor pair

anchor.snippet: are related in a natural manner

annotations.note: The compatible naturality of Hom(A,–) and Hom(–,B) is precisely the functoriality witnessed by the single bifunctor `Functor.hom : Cᵒᵖ × C ⥤ Type`.

annotations.label: Hom(–,–) is a bifunctor

anchor.snippet: implies that Hom(–, –) is a bifunctor from

annotations.note: `Functor.hom : Cᵒᵖ × C ⥤ Type v` is the hom bifunctor, contravariant in the first and covariant in the second argument.

annotations.label: Yoneda's lemma

anchor.snippet: every natural transformation between Hom functors is of this form

annotations.note: `yonedaEquiv : (yoneda.obj X ⟶ F) ≃ F.obj (op X)` is the Yoneda lemma, with its naturality packaged as `yonedaLemma`.

annotations.label: Internal Hom functor

anchor.snippet: Such a functor is referred to as the internal Hom functor

annotations.note: `internalHom : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ C ⥤ C` (built from `ihom`) is the internal hom bifunctor of a monoidal closed category.

annotations.label: Closed category

anchor.snippet: are referred to as closed categories

annotations.note: `MonoidalClosed C` is the closed (monoidal) category structure the article refers to as a closed category.

annotations.label: Currying / internal Hom adjunction

anchor.snippet: this extends to the notion of currying

annotations.note: `ihom.adjunction A : tensorLeft A ⊣ ihom A` together with `MonoidalClosed.curry`/`uncurry` formalizes currying as the internal hom adjunction.

annotations.label: Internal Hom object

anchor.snippet: is called the internal Hom

annotations.note: The internal hom object is `(ihom A).obj X`, with notation `A ⟶[C] X`.

annotations.label: Exponential object

anchor.snippet: is called the exponential object

annotations.note: In the cartesian closed case the exponential object is `(ihom A).obj B` with `CartesianClosed` notations `A ⟹ B` and `B ^^ A`.

annotations.label: Internal language of a category

anchor.snippet: form a language, called the internal language of the category

annotations.note: Mathlib does not formalize the internal language / type theory of a (closed) category.

annotations.label: Presheaf and copresheaf

anchor.snippet: is a presheaf ; likewise, Hom( A , –) is a copresheaf

annotations.note: Mathlib represents (co)presheaves on a general category as the literal types `Cᵒᵖ ⥤ Type`/`C ⥤ Type` (e.g. the codomain of `yoneda`) without a dedicated `Presheaf C` abbreviation.

annotations.label: Representable functor

anchor.snippet: is called a representable functor

annotations.note: `Functor.IsRepresentable` (with `Functor.RepresentableBy`) defines a functor isomorphic to a hom functor.

annotations.label: Hom is the identity profunctor

anchor.snippet: is a profunctor , and, specifically, it is the identity profunctor

annotations.note: `Profunctor.id C` is defined to be the Yoneda/hom bifunctor, exactly the identity profunctor on C.

annotations.label: Internal Hom preserves limits

anchor.snippet: The internal hom functor preserves limits

annotations.note: `ihom A` is a right adjoint (`(ihom A).IsRightAdjoint` via `ihom.adjunction`), so it preserves limits by `Adjunction.rightAdjoint_preservesLimits`.

annotations.label: Reader (environment) monad

anchor.snippet: can be given the structure of a monad

annotations.note: The reader/environment monad (`ReaderT`/`ReaderM`) lives in Lean core, and the categorical statement that Hom(A,–) carries a monad structure is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hom left-exact, exact iff projective

anchor.snippet: is a covariant left-exact functor from

annotations.note: `coyoneda.obj (op P)` always preserves limits (`coyoneda_preservesLimits`, i.e. left-exactness), and `projective_iff_preservesEpimorphisms_coyoneda_obj` gives `Projective P ↔ Hom(P,–) preserves epis` for general categories.

annotations.label: Hom adjoint to tensor product

anchor.snippet: is adjoint to the tensor product functor

annotations.note: `TensorProduct.lift.equiv : (M →ₗ N →ₗ P) ≃ₗ (M ⊗ N →ₗ P)` is the tensor–hom adjunction, also packaged categorically via `MonoidalClosed (ModuleCat R)`.
